title: Infra mocks: pick a serving design + answer cost / SLO trade-offs
focus: Self-mock 30-min: 'serve a 70B model at 100 QPS, $X budget, P99 < Y'.

items.label: Model servers: TorchServe, Triton, BentoML, Seldon
interviewQuestions: Compare TorchServe, Triton, and BentoML — when does each fit?
What is dynamic batching and why does it matter for GPU utilization?

items.label: Containerization & Kubernetes for ML
interviewQuestions: Walk through deploying a model on Kubernetes with autoscaling.
When do you reach for KServe vs custom Deployment + HPA?

items.label: Traffic routing, model rollback, A/B serving
interviewQuestions: How would you do a safe rollback when a new model regresses online metrics?
What guardrail metrics would automatically trigger a rollback without a human in the loop?

items.label: Quantization: INT8, INT4, FP8, GPTQ, AWQ
interviewQuestions: Compare post-training quantization (PTQ) vs quantization-aware training (QAT).
How do GPTQ and AWQ work, and what quality do you lose?

items.label: Knowledge distillation (teacher → student)
interviewQuestions: Walk me through knowledge distillation — what is the soft-target loss?
Why does temperature in the soft target matter, and how do you pick it?

items.label: Pruning: structured vs unstructured
interviewQuestions: Compare structured vs unstructured pruning — which actually speeds up inference?
Why does unstructured pruning rarely move latency on GPU, even when sparsity is high?

items.label: Continuous batching & dynamic batching
interviewQuestions: How does continuous batching beat static batching for LLM serving?
What's the trade-off between max batch size and per-request latency under continuous batching?
Compare TorchServe, Triton, and BentoML — when does each fit?
What is dynamic batching, and why does it matter for GPU utilization?
Walk through deploying a model on Kubernetes with autoscaling — what knobs matter?
